bootstrap.note: Bootstrap degraded because the observed manifest drifted after assimilation; this correctly blocks authority claims.

self_host_lifecycle.reason: The benchmark performs git clone --no-local from the active Cortex folder; the NEXUS vendored copy excludes .git.

worked_well: Cortex indexed the NEXUS repository with full eligible-file coverage.
Thalamus routing improved relevant target rank and top-3 recall on the synthetic workload.
Nexus packet output is fast enough to become a practical rehydration surface.
Governor correctly stayed read-only when manifest drift made the bootstrap certificate degraded.
improvements: Add a NEXUS cortex lane and compiler gate.
Add manifest refresh/resume so degraded bootstrap can self-explain the exact stale inputs.
Add a report-pruning policy so Cortex ignores generated evidence churn by default.
Expose Cortex status and activated paths in Electron as read-only cards.
Run self-host lifecycle from the original Git-backed Cortex repository or keep a Git metadata stub for benchmark-only validation.

claim_boundary: Cortex benchmarks are local development evidence only. They do not prove correctness, safety, security, production readiness, biological fidelity, model understanding, or autonomous authority.
